title: Refried Butternut Tacos
subtitle: with Roasted Grape Salsa and Plantains
description: Our latest taco dish provides the perfect balance of sweet and spicy. Fragrant grapes and twice-cooked butternut squash complement flavorful chipotle and jalapeño. You'll enjoy your tacos with crispy plantains on the side.

steps.title: COOK THE BUTTERNUT
steps.text: Preheat oven to 400°F. Add the butternut squash to a small saucepan and cover with at least 1 inch of cold water. Bring to a boil, reduce heat to medium, and boil squash until tender (you should be able to pierce the flesh easily with a fork), about 11 to 13 minutes. Drain squash, return to the saucepan, and mash with a fork until smooth.

steps.title: ROAST THE GRAPES
steps.text: Halve the grapes. Place on a baking sheet and toss with 1 tsp olive oil and a pinch of salt and pepper. Roast until grapes are soft, fragrant, and slightly wrinkled, about 6 to 8 minutes. Let cool for at least 5 minutes.

steps.title: PREPARE THE SALSA
steps.text: Deseed and mince the jalapeño. Thinly slice the scallion. Finely chop the cilantro leaves. Combine everything in a medium bowl and add the juice from half the lime. Once the grapes have cooled slightly, add to the bowl along with a pinch of salt and pepper. Mix salsa well to combine. Cut remaining lime half into wedges. Peel the plantain and slice into 1 inch thick slices on the diagonal.

steps.title: FRY THE BUTTERNUT
steps.text: Place a large nonstick skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tbsp olive oil. Add the mashed butternut and press into a single layer in the skillet. Lower heat to medium and cook, undisturbed, until bottom is well browned, about 5 to 8 minutes. Stir, then smooth squash into an even layer once again, and cook for 5 minutes. Stir in just 1 tsp chipotle hot sauce, butter, and agave. Season with salt.

steps.title: CRISP THE PLANTAINS
steps.text: Wrap the flour tortillas in foil or a dish towel and warm them in the oven for about 5 minutes. Place a medium nonstick skillet over medium-high heat with 1 tbsp vegetable oil. Once the oil is hot, add the sliced plantains and cook until browned on one side, about 3 to 4 minutes. Flip, sprinkle with salt, and cook until browned on the other side, another 3 to 4 minutes.

steps.title: BUILD YOUR TACOS
steps.text: Cut remaining lime half into wedges. Divide the refried butternut between the warm tortillas, spreading it out a bit. Add the roasted grape salsa and green cabbage. Drizzle on any remaining chipotle hot sauce and serve with crispy plantains and lime wedges. Dig in!

tips: *This ingredient will be split between multiple steps.
TIP: We will use the oven again, don't turn it off yet!
TIP: Brown spots are normal on plantains—they indicate sweetness.
TIP: Lower heat as needed to prevent squash from burning.
